text: There was no European regular trading session on the report date, Saturday, June 20, 2026. Euronext states its cash and derivatives markets are open Monday to Friday throughout 2026, and the London Stock Exchange operates its trading services on weekdays, so no European cash equities traded on June 20; the FTSE 100 historical daily table ends at Friday, June 19, with no weekend row.

value.note: Europe traded normally on June 19; the Juneteenth holiday closed only US markets, not European venues

market_session: last European regular session before the report-date weekend
text: The last European regular session was Friday, June 19, 2026, a normal trading day for European venues. Juneteenth is a US-only market holiday, so while Wall Street was shut on June 19, European exchanges traded as usual; their standing levels therefore date to the June 19 close, one session more recent than the last US close.

stoxx_europe_600.basis: Trading Economics rounded level; Reuters and CNBC both report -0.2%

euro_stoxx_50.basis: Trading Economics EU50 page shows a -0.3% dated wrap (6,302) and a -0.56% CFD settle (6,288) for June 19; vendor basis disclosed

dax.basis: Trading Economics DE40 settle 24,986/-0.16%; CNBC described the DAX as finishing flat

ftse_100.basis: Investors Chronicle/LSEG precise historical close; CNBC -0.35%

market_session: European regular close on 2026-06-19 (last session before the report-date weekend); FTSE 100 at the 16:35 BST London close

calculation.method: FTSE 100: June 19 close 10,363.27 minus June 18 close 10,399.70 equals -36.43 points (-0.35%); continental levels are vendor-reported June 19 closes with percent moves corroborated across Reuters, CNBC and Trading Economics

text: Carried as standing levels into the weekend, the June 19 European close was modestly lower across the board: the STOXX Europe 600 about 636 (-0.2%), the EURO STOXX 50 about 6,302 on Trading Economics's dated wrap (-0.3%, or 6,288/-0.56% on its CFD settle), the DAX about 24,986 (-0.16%, described as flat by CNBC), the CAC 40 about 8,421 (-0.55%), and the FTSE 100 precisely 10,363.27, down 36.43 points (-0.35%) from 10,399.70. These are last-session levels, not a June 20 print.

ftse_100.basis: calculated from Investors Chronicle/LSEG closes: 10,363.27 vs prior-Friday 10,471.72

market_session: weekly change for the week ended Friday 2026-06-19

calculation.method: FTSE 100 weekly: June 19 close 10,363.27 vs June 12 close 10,471.72 equals -108.45 points, -1.04% (about -1.0%); continental weekly moves as reported by Trading Economics and Reuters

text: For the week ended June 19, the continental indices still finished higher despite Friday's dip: the STOXX Europe 600 about +0.4%, the EURO STOXX 50 about +1.9%, and the DAX about +1.6%. The FTSE 100 lagged, down about 1.0% on the week (10,363.27 versus the prior Friday's 10,471.72). These are last-week figures carried into the weekend, not report-date moves.

subject.name: last-session risk-off, geopolitics-led pullback from record highs

value.primary_pressure: scheduled US-Iran talks in Switzerland called off as fighting flared in Lebanon; Israel and Hezbollah later agreed a ceasefire
value.us_session: no US trading on June 19 - Juneteenth federal holiday
value.ecb: Pierre Wunsch said the ECB could raise rates again as soon as next month if inflation broadened beyond energy

market_session: European regular session, Friday June 19, 2026 (carried as last-session context)
text: The driver of the last European session was geopolitical: European shares inched lower from record highs after scheduled US-Iran talks in Switzerland were called off amid fresh fighting in Lebanon (Israel and Hezbollah later agreed a ceasefire), with US markets shut for Juneteenth. Oil and gas stocks led gains while miners and travel lagged. Luxury fell (LVMH -1.2%, L'Oreal -1.9%, Hermes -2.3%) alongside tech (ASML -1%, Prosus -2.2%); Volkswagen dropped more than 4% ex-dividend while German defense names (Rheinmetall +1.8%, Renk +1.5%, Hensoldt +1.5%) and Mercedes-Benz (+1.9%, on reported EU tariffs on Chinese plug-in hybrids) gained. In France, Airbus (-1.3%) and Safran (-1.2%) fell while TotalEnergies rose about 1%. The ECB's Pierre Wunsch said the bank could hike again as soon as next month if inflation broadened beyond energy.

market_session: next European regular session after the weekend
text: European equity markets next open for regular trading on Monday, June 22, 2026, the first weekday after the report-date weekend, consistent with the Monday-to-Friday trading calendars published by Euronext and the London Stock Exchange.

euronext_core.note: December 24 is a half trading day

market_session: next scheduled European equity-market closures after the report-date weekend
text: Unlike the US, European venues follow separate national holiday calendars, so there is no single next closure. After June 19, the next London Stock Exchange non-trading day is Monday, August 31, 2026 (Summer Bank Holiday), while the next full closure for Euronext's core markets (Paris, Amsterdam, Brussels) is Friday, December 25, 2026 (Christmas Day), with December 24 a half trading day.
